statement: In Romans 3:24, the verb δικαιόω ("dikaioumenoi" / "being justified") is used primarily in a forensic, declarative sense (God's legal declaration of righteousness), rather than in a primarily transformative or intrinsic-renewal sense.

passages.original_text: dikaioumenoi dwrean th autou cariti dia thv apolutrwsewv thv en cristw ihsou

translations.KJV-1769: Being justified freely by his grace through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus:

passages.original_text: nuni de cwriv nomou dikaiosunh yeou pefanerwtai marturoumenh upo tou nomou kai twn profhtwn ... dikaioumenoi dwrean th autou cariti dia thv apolutrwsewv thv en cristw ihsou ... eiv to einai auton dikaion kai dikaiounta ton ek pistewv ihsou

translations.KJV-1769: But now the righteousness of God without the law is manifested, being witnessed by the law and the prophets; ... Being justified freely by his grace through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus: ... To declare, I say, at this time his righteousness: that he might be just, and the justifier of him which believeth in Jesus.

version_history.note: Initial creation of atomic claim definition for Romans 3:24 δικαιόω (forensic vs. transformative sense) using TR-Scrivener-1894 and KJV-1769 Romans 3 corpora.
